# Md Shafaat Ali Choyon 

> Growth, marketing and public-health strategist with 16+ years turning complex systems into measurable results across telecom, advertising, fintech, e-commerce, consumer tech, education and healthcare. MBA · MCIM · MPH · CHES®. Based in Redford, Michigan, USA.

## About

Md Shafaat Ali Choyon (also written Shafaat Choyon) is a strategist who works at the intersection of growth marketing, health communication, digital-health product, and population health. He holds a Master of Public Health (Health Education & Promotion) and is CHES®-credentialed, holds an MBA, and is a Member of the Chartered Institute of Marketing (MCIM). His portfolio is framed through four lenses — Growth & Marketing, Health Communication, Health-Tech Product, and Population Health — each presenting the same body of work for a different audience.

## The through-line

His 16+ years move through three modes of value: winning **attention** (agency and brand work — a No. 1-globally product launch, a No. 1 marketplace category built in 2.5 months), building the **intelligence** behind decisions (data-led growth, BI housed inside marketing, 65–70% of decisions made on data), and owning measurable **outcomes** (growth, access, retention, public-health impact). His operating principle: "Read the system, find the lever, move the number."

## Areas of expertise

- Growth marketing and brand strategy
- Health communication and behavior-change campaigns
- Public health, population health, health literacy, and health policy
- Digital-health products and platform adoption
- Multi-channel campaigns and product marketing
- Operations, data and transformation (ERP/BI, ARPU/CLV/cohorting)

## Measurable results

- Praava Health: ~57% B2C CAGR (2021–2023); corporate clients grown 342 → ~1,400; lab margin ~30% → ~50%; roughly 2% of Bangladesh's population (~3.6 million people) reached during the COVID pandemic
- Samsung: Galaxy Note9 launch recognized No. 1 globally for growth (1.54x year-on-year)
- SureCash: 10-million-beneficiary government-to-person (G2P) stipend rollout; Banking Fair Award of Excellence
- Laser Treat Group: $0.5M operating-cost reduction and +13% patient retention via ERP/BI digitization
- Ekhanei.com: No. 1 motorbike category built in 2.5 months (+25% revenue, +15% market share)
- MPH Capstone: documented ~3x reach amplification on a multi-platform public-health campaign

## Selected experience

- Praava Health — Head of Marketing & Corporate Sales (Head of Consumer Business); 83% of revenue P&L, 30-person org
- Laser Treat Group — Chief Operating Officer (clinical operations and data transformation)
- Samsung, Asiatic (JWT), Grey/WPP, MGH, Grameenphone, SureCash, Ekhanei, ACCA — marketing, brand, product and campaign leadership across consumer tech, advertising, fintech, e-commerce and professional services
- Eastern Michigan University — Graduate Assistant (Health Promotion), Graduate Hall Director, and MPH candidate; public-health campaigns and evaluation

## Published writing

14 published health-policy and digital-health articles in outlets including The Eastern Echo, The Daily Star, Dhaka Tribune, IBT, and The Financial Express. Topics include the public-health workforce, health communication, health literacy, medical debt, public-health investment, misinformation, and digital health and the economy.

## In the press (independent coverage)

19 verified third-party mentions across 12 outlets (TV, print and digital), including:

- Appointment coverage: "Laser Treat names Md Shafaat Ali Choyon as new COO" (Dhaka Tribune, Nov 2023); "Shafaat becomes Laser Treat COO" (New Age, Nov 2023)
- TV & interviews: 19-minute live interview on ATN News "Happening Point" (May 2023); feature interview on business digitization in Bangladesh Pratidin (May 2021)
- Praava Health era: quoted launch spokesperson for the "Ghore Lab" home-diagnostics launch (The Business Standard, Feb 2023); signed strategic partnerships covered by The Business Standard (Guardian Life, Bangladesh Finance) and BEXIMCO's BOL; named among senior leadership in 5th- and 6th-anniversary coverage (The Daily Star, The Business Standard, Dhaka Tribune); named speaker at the Beauty & Wellness Centre launch (The Daily Star, Dec 2021)
- ACCA era: roundtable panelist quoted in The Daily Star's "The business partner of the future" with PwC Bangladesh (Dec 2019); named keynote at national certification ceremonies (The Financial Express, The Daily Observer, Feb 2020); named at the ACCA–BTEB MoU signing (Prothom Alo, May 2021)
- Ekhanei Bike Bazaar (2017): the first-of-its-kind second-hand motorbike fair he led was covered by The Daily Star, an ATN News "Young Nite" TV special, and GTV / Independent TV news bulletins

Full list with links: https://shafaatalichoyon.com/#press

## HSREP — Health System Resilience & Economic Protection (founder)

Md Shafaat Ali Choyon is the founder of HSREP (https://hsraep.org), an independent policy-research and advocacy platform arguing that health protection is economic infrastructure, not a residual budget line. Season 1 was an eight-week, two-country (US and Bangladesh) campaign of nine published pieces that reached 57,274 people at $0 ad spend (+3,536% LinkedIn and +2,121% Facebook reach, cold-start), with a locked APA-7 analytics framework and four replicable findings on how health arguments travel across platforms and borders. HSREP is developing the Prevention Adoption Initiative — a proposed colorectal-cancer screening completion pilot for safety-net settings in Southeast Michigan, with FHIR/TEFCA-ready dashboards and human-in-the-loop, fairness-audited AI outreach. Platform: https://hsraep.org

## Essays (original, on-site)

59 original essays — dual-market field notes (US & Bangladesh) on AI, public health, health-tech and growth. Full filterable index: https://shafaatalichoyon.com/essays.html

- [Building in public with AI — what I learned running my own](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/building-in-public-with-ai.html) — I didn't just write about AI this quarter — I ran it. A system built for my own work: 155 sessions, zero fabricated facts, verification built in from the start.
- [Aging happens fast now — before the systems are ready](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/aging-happens-fast.html) — Bangladesh's over-60s nearly double by 2050 with no home-care model; the US market tops $250B. Elder care is the next market almost nobody's building yet.
- [The authenticity dividend - your last real moat](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/authenticity-dividend.html) — In a feed drowning in AI sameness, the scarce thing is proof a person made it. Notes on trust — from Dhaka's markets to the backlash against AI slop.
- [Voice-first AI for the next billion](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/voice-first-ai.html) — The last billion online won't type — they'll talk. Voice skips the two barriers keeping AI from those who need it most: literacy and the keyboard.
- [The slow pandemic — antimicrobial resistance](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/amr-slow-pandemic.html) — Antimicrobial resistance may kill 39 million by 2050 — three a minute, South Asia hardest hit. A behavior and communication problem, and so it's neglected.
- [AI can translate words, not localize culture](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/ai-cant-localize-culture.html) — AI dubbing cuts localization cost 70-90%; translation is nearly free. But translating words isn't localizing culture — and that gap is where judgment wins.
- [The pharmacy is the front door of care](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/pharmacy-front-door.html) — In Bangladesh care begins at the pharmacist; in the US ~50M live in a pharmacy desert. Same underused node — the pharmacy as health's real front door.
- [Commerce runs on chat, not storefronts](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/social-commerce-growth.html) — Bangladesh runs one of the world's largest F-commerce ecosystems — 300,000+ sellers working from a chat thread. People buy from a person, not a store.
- [The dual-market operator's edge](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/dual-market-operator.html) — Most operators compete in one market. I read two — the US and Bangladesh — daily. Sixteen years taught me that bridge is a position no one else can occupy.
- [The nutrition transition: the environment is the disease](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/nutrition-transition.html) — Bangladesh now carries undernutrition and diabetes at once; the US drowns in the same processed calories. The food environment, not willpower, is the disease.
- [The model's blind spot is your opportunity](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-models-blind-spot.html) — AI models learn from a lopsided web. Every language and market it underrepresents is where models are weak — and where you can win. The gap is the opening.
- [Femtech: health's clearest open market](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/femtech-open-market.html) — Women's health is worth tens of billions, yet barely 4% of biopharma R&D addressed it. The under-served half of the population is health's clearest open market.
- [Create the category, don't compete in one](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/create-the-category.html) — Positioning wins a slot someone else defined. The stronger move: design the category itself — name the problem, set the terms, become the default answer.
- [The remittance-to-health pipeline](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/remittance-health-pipeline.html) — Bangladesh took in a record $35.56B in remittances while ~70M face health hardship. Connect them: the country's biggest untapped health-financing rail.
- [The mosquito economy — dengue is a climate problem now](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/dengue-climate-health.html) — Bangladesh logged 102,000+ dengue cases last year; 2026 is climbing. It's no longer only ours — local dengue is turning up in the US. Climate rewrote the map.
- [The call-center cliff and AI voice agents](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-call-center-cliff.html) — AI voice agents are coming for the call-center economy — Bangladesh built ~$850M and 85,000 jobs on it. Can we move people up the value chain fast enough?
- [Trust in the pill — the counterfeit-medicine problem](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/trust-in-the-pill.html) — One in ten medicines in poorer countries is substandard or fake — a trust collapse you engineer out of the supply. Rich-world shortages open the same door.
- [Measure the number you're avoiding](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/measure-what-you-avoid.html) — Every team measures something. The winning ones measure the number they'd rather not look at — and decide what to count before they start, not after.
- [The quiet epidemic — tobacco and the new nicotine](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-quiet-epidemic.html) — Tobacco kills ~158,000 a year in Bangladesh — one in five deaths; the US fight moved to youth vaping. Same economy, opposite framing — shaming never wins it.
- [Roads are a health crisis we refuse to own](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/roads-are-a-health-crisis.html) — Bangladesh had its deadliest road year in 2025 — 9,000+ lives — while the US cut its toll. Same machine, opposite paths: one treats road death as designable.
- [When the buyer is an AI, not a person](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/when-the-buyer-is-an-ai.html) — Google's "Buy for me" and ChatGPT Shopping mean the next shopper is an agent, not a person. Agentic commerce may hit $3-5T — few brands are built for it.
- [The cold chain is the product, not the pill](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/cold-chain-is-the-product.html) — US drug shortages hit 223 in early 2026, lasting five-plus years. For medicines, logistics is the health outcome — and whoever owns the last cold inch wins.
- [Pricing is positioning, not a spreadsheet](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/pricing-is-positioning.html) — Your price is the loudest thing your brand says. In cash markets where every taka is felt, pricing is your sharpest positioning decision — not a spreadsheet.
- [The $200M question — where to spend it on health](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/two-hundred-million-question.html) — The World Bank put $200M into Bangladesh's primary care. Given real money, where does a health dollar actually change an outcome? My answer isn't a shiny app.
- [Clean hands, still — the cheapest health tech we underuse](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/clean-hands-still.html) — Bangladesh hit 98% water access, yet arsenic poisons ~50M; the US is still pulling 9M lead pipes. Water and hygiene: the highest-return health tech, underused.
- [The lab is the wedge — diagnostics-led care](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/diagnostics-led-care.html) — In cash-pay markets the cheapest way into care isn't a doctor — it's a test. I built care that led with the lab, the model richer markets rediscover at home.
- [Air quality is a health emergency nobody owns](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/air-quality-health-emergency.html) — Dhaka's air ranks among the world's worst — PM2.5 ~13x the WHO limit — and US wildfire smoke made the threat American. High-leverage, and no one owns it.
- [Financing the uninsured — the missing rail in health](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/financing-the-uninsured.html) — In Bangladesh, out-of-pocket costs are ~68.5% of health spending; ~70M face hardship. The fix isn't a clinic — it's a payment rail I built at national scale.
- [The first 1,000 days — the highest-ROI window in health](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-first-1000-days.html) — Bangladesh cut maternal deaths 79%; the US is the only developed country where they rise. Conception to age two is where a health dollar does the most work.
- [Frugal innovation as a design philosophy](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/frugal-innovation-philosophy.html) — The West calls it "frugal innovation." In Bangladesh we called it getting it done. The design principles of 2026 are what scarcity taught us years ago.
- [The last mile - health tech's untapped value](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-last-mile.html) — Most health tech serves the first mile — the hospital. The untapped value is the last mile: the person far from care, reached where they are.
- [Clarity is an intervention, not polish](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/clarity-is-an-intervention.html) — We treat clarity as polish. It isn't. Confusing health guidance has a body count and a price tag — clarity is itself a health intervention.
- [The personal-brand playbook for the markets everyone ignores](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/personal-brand-playbook.html) — The most underrated growth asset in 2026 isn't a company brand — it's a person's. And the biggest opening to build one is where Western creators aren't looking.
- [The trust cold-start in two-sided health platforms](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-trust-cold-start.html) — Every platform faces the same first problem: nobody comes because nobody's there. In health it's worse — the currency isn't supply and demand, it's trust.
- [The workforce crisis and the community-health-worker answer](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/workforce-crisis-chw-answer.html) — America's health system is short of people, not technology. The answer is a model Bangladesh has run for decades — move tasks to those closest to the community.
- [The consent problem - health AI's real bottleneck](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/the-consent-problem.html) — Every debate about health AI fixates on accuracy. What actually stalls it is trust by design — consent, privacy, and provenance — the layer nobody demos.
- [Everyone says "AI-powered" - position on the job](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/everyone-says-ai-powered.html) — When every booth claims the same edge, the phrase means nothing. Positioning must move where copycats can't follow — the job the customer is hiring you to do.
- [Prevention-first — the design window that closes](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/prevention-first-window.html) — The US didn't choose sick-care — it backed into what the money rewarded. A market still building its system has an option the US lost: put prevention first.
- [How mental health became the premium category](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/category-nobody-would-fund.html) — Investors once treated mental health as charity. In 2026 a mental-health app sold for $865M. The demand was always there — someone designed around the barrier.
- [After the scribe: where health AI goes next](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/after-the-scribe.html) — The AI scribe was the easy win. The real 2026 question is what AI does after the note — in the back office where the money and the misery live.
- [Stop throwing away what you publish](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/stop-throwing-away-what-you-publish.html) — The most wasteful thing in content isn't a bad idea — it's a good one you published once and dropped. Atomize a few durable assets instead of churning posts.
- [Telehealth's second act — care that comes to you](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/telehealth-second-act.html) — Telehealth's first act was pandemic video for the waiting room. The second act is less a video call and more care delivered to your home, on your schedule.
- [Shame is a design flaw — not a personal failing](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/shame-is-a-design-flaw.html) — Shame feels like clarity but works like a wall. In health comms, stigma is a barrier you design around — channel, framing, segmentation — not lecture away.
- [Provenance over volume - AI's next health win](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/provenance-over-volume.html) — The AI health rush chases the wrong verb. Everyone races to generate more; the scarce thing was never volume — it's proving what's true and where it came from.
- [Retention is the real growth engine](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/retention-is-the-real-growth.html) — Acquisition costs rose ~60% in five years. Winners obsess over the leaky bucket — cash markets taught me retention, not reach, is the growth engine.
- [When patients pay cash, everything about the product changes](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/when-patients-pay-cash.html) — In the US, health products are built for a payer. In most of the world the patient pays — and that fact is a design discipline the US is drifting toward.
- [A hotline on a bar of soap - distribution wins](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/hotline-on-a-bar-of-soap.html) — The best health channel I ever used wasn't an app or an ad — it was a bar of soap. Putting a clinic inside a product people owned took ~3,000 calls a day.
- [AI for the two-person Dhaka business](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/ai-for-dhaka-sme.html) — Everyone tells the enterprise AI story. The one that matters: a two-person Dhaka shop running like a twenty-person one — an SME's first affordable team.
- [When nobody trusts anybody, trust is the growth strategy](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/trust-is-the-growth-strategy.html) — In low-trust categories, trust isn't a brand value — it's the growth engine. From moving 10 million onto digital payments where trust was the entire barrier.
- [The middle is disappearing: digital health's $4B quarter](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/digital-health-middle-disappearing.html) — Digital health raised $4B last quarter and 12 companies took 59%. What that concentration and "AI as table stakes" mean for everyone who isn't a megadeal.
- [GLP-1s: the drug was never the hard part](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/glp1-behavior-not-biology.html) — Only ~12% of Americans take a GLP-1, and most who start quit. Pharma solved the biology; behavior and adherence decide the outcome — the layer I've built on.
- [The agentic solo operator: a team of one](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/agentic-solo-operator.html) — Agentic AI removed the ceiling on what one operator can ship. Field notes from running a full growth engine solo — where judgment, not labor, is the limit.
- [The pendulum swings back to brand](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/brand-vs-performance.html) — Performance marketers report diminishing returns, ad spend passed $1T, and AI floods every channel with sameness. The scarce asset: a brand people trust.
- [Where health AI actually lands by 2027 — a builder's bet](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/health-ai-2027-bet.html) — Adoption is done. Three bets on where health AI actually lands by 2027 — people over models, judgment, and plumbing — each grounded in work I've shipped.
- [I rebuilt my website to be read by AI, not just Google](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/built-for-ai-not-google.html) — 80% of searches now end without a click. Field notes on optimizing for answer engines — structured data, llms.txt, and becoming the source AI actually cites.
- [The healthtech leapfrog Bangladesh already made](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/healthtech-leapfrog.html) — Bangladesh jumped from cash straight to mobile money. In health tech the same leapfrog is the opening — build access-first, not a copy of the US. I've run it.
- [Measles is back — a trust problem, not a supply problem](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/measles-trust-not-supply.html) — The US passed 2,200 measles cases in 2026 — a trust problem, not supply. Why messengers beat facts, from a soap-packet hotline in Dhaka to a US campus campaign.
- [Bangla-first AI: the market the giants keep skipping](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/bangla-first-ai.html) — Bengali is a top-seven world language and barely a fraction of the web. A builder's dual-market case for the opportunity the giants overlook.
- [Health communication that changes behavior](https://shafaatalichoyon.com/health-communication-behavior-change.html) — Field notes on segmentation, friction, framing and measurement — the principles behind campaigns that move behavior, not just awareness.
